---
title: "Final"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-28"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

# Imports
```{r}
library(quantmod)
library(tidyverse)
library(tseries)
library(urca)
library(forecast)
library(lubridate)
library(gridExtra)
```
# Load data
```{r}
start_date <- as.Date("2016-01-01")

# 1) Pull Data
getSymbols("BTC-USD", src="yahoo", from=start_date)
btc <- Cl(`BTC-USD`)
colnames(btc) <- "BTC"

getSymbols("SPY", src="yahoo", from=start_date)
spy <- Cl(SPY)
colnames(spy) <- "SPY"

getSymbols("QQQ", src="yahoo", from=start_date)
qqq <- Cl(QQQ)
colnames(qqq) <- "QQQ"

getSymbols("DFF", src="FRED")
fed <- DFF
colnames(fed) <- "FedFunds"

getSymbols("CPIAUCSL", src="FRED")
cpi <- CPIAUCSL
colnames(cpi) <- "CPI"

getSymbols("DGS10", src="FRED")
dgs10 <- DGS10
colnames(dgs10) <- "DGS10"

daily_data <- merge(btc, spy, qqq, fed, dgs10, cpi)

# Restrict to start date
daily_data <- daily_data[index(daily_data) >= start_date]

# Create full daily date sequence
full_dates <- seq(from = min(index(daily_data)),
                  to   = max(index(daily_data)),
                  by   = "day")

daily_data <- merge(daily_data,
                    xts(order.by = full_dates),
                    fill = NA)

# Forward-fill missing values
daily_data <- na.locf(daily_data, na.rm = FALSE)

# Backward-fill very first missing values
daily_data <- na.locf(daily_data, fromLast = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# Inspect Dataset
head(daily_data)
tail(daily_data)
dim(daily_data)
```

```{r}
summary(cars)
```

```{r}
summary(cars)
```

```{r}
summary(cars)
```

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```

```{r cars}
summary(cars)
```
